# UC-15.1.28 · APC UPS Output Load Percent Approaching Nameplate Limit

## Description

`upsAdvOutputLoad` reports UPS load as a percent of rated capacity. Sustained high load reduces available runtime and accelerates thermal stress. Trending 30-minute averages above 80% with peaks beyond 90% triggers capacity reviews before overload alarms or breaker trips during transfer events.

## Value

Avoids cascading outages by proving when a UPS is out of runway before the next rack install lands on the same circuit.

## Implementation

Poll every 1–5 minutes. Confirm field extraction matches your SNMP translator (`upsAdvOutputLoad` vs `upsHighPrecOutputLoadPercent` on high-precision models). Maintain a lookup for rated kVA if you need to convert to kW for finance. Alert on sustained `avg_load > 80` for 2 intervals or instantaneous `peak_load > 90`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• DCIM linkage from UPS host to rack IDs.
• Exclude maintenance bypass states via `upsBasicOutputStatus` if available.

Step 1 — Validate SNMP values against NMC web UI.

Step 2 — Forecast load for planned installs.

Step 3 — Pair with PDU outlet UC-15.1.7 for attribution.

Step 4 — Change board approval when >85% sustained.

## SPL

```spl
index=power sourcetype=snmp:apc:ups host=*
| eval load_pct=tonumber(upsAdvOutputLoad)
| bin _time span=30m
| stats avg(load_pct) as avg_load, max(load_pct) as peak_load by _time, host
| where avg_load > 80 OR peak_load > 90
```

## Visualization

Line chart (load trend), Gauge (current %), Bar chart (top-loaded UPS).
